description: Create the complete Flutter translator app with all source code, configuration, and CI/CD pipeline. This is a greenfield project so everything is created in one coherent feature.
steps: Step 1: Create pubspec.yaml with dependencies: http, flutter_tts, speech_to_text, provider, and dev dependencies for testing

Step 2: Create lib/models/language.dart - Language model class with code, name, and a static list of all supported languages (use MyMemory/standard ISO 639-1 codes). Include at minimum: English, Vietnamese, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Russian, Japanese, Korean, Chinese (Simplified), Chinese (Traditional), Thai, Arabic, Hindi, Indonesian, Malay, Dutch, Polish, Turkish, Swedish, Czech, Danish, Finnish, Greek, Hebrew, Hungarian, Norwegian, Romanian, Ukrainian

Step 3: Create lib/services/translation_service.dart - Service that calls MyMemory API (https://api.mymemory.translated.net/get?q={text}&langpair={from}|{to}), parses JSON response, returns translated text. Handle errors gracefully.
Step 4: Create lib/services/tts_service.dart - Text-to-Speech service using flutter_tts package. Methods: speak(text, languageCode), stop(). Configure speech rate and pitch.
Step 5: Create lib/services/speech_service.dart - Speech-to-Text service using speech_to_text package. Methods: initialize(), startListening(onResult, localeId), stopListening(). Handle permissions.

Step 6: Create lib/widgets/speak_button.dart - Large circular animated microphone button (Material Design 3). Should pulse/animate when listening. Use AnimationController for a breathing/pulse effect. Size: at least 80px radius.
Step 7: Create lib/widgets/language_selector.dart - Dropdown widget for selecting languages. Shows language name, supports search/filter if many languages.

Step 8: Create lib/screens/translator_screen.dart - Main screen with: language selectors at top (with swap button), text input field, translated text display, large speak button in center, TTS buttons for both source and target text. Use Material Design 3 theming with a nice color scheme (deep purple/teal gradient accents).
Step 9: Create lib/main.dart - App entry point with MaterialApp, Material Design 3 theme (useMaterial3: true), color scheme with seed color, and TranslatorScreen as home.
Step 10: Create analysis_options.yaml with standard Flutter linting rules
Step 11: Create web/index.html and web/manifest.json for web platform support
Step 12: Create test/translation_service_test.dart - Unit test for translation service (mock HTTP, verify parsing)

Step 13: Create .github/workflows/build.yml - GitHub Actions workflow that: triggers on push to main, sets up Flutter, builds Web (zip), Android APK (release), Windows EXE (release), then creates a GitHub Release with all artifacts attached. Use subosito/flutter-action@v2. For Android, set up Java 17. For Windows, use windows-latest runner. Use a matrix or sequential jobs. Tag releases with a timestamp or run number.
Step 14: Create .gitignore for Flutter project (build/, .dart_tool/, etc.)
Step 15: Create android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml with INTERNET and RECORD_AUDIO permissions

Step 16: Create all necessary Android Gradle build files (android/build.gradle, android/app/build.gradle, android/settings.gradle, android/gradle.properties, android/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties) configured for release APK build without signing
Step 17: Create windows/runner/ files needed for Windows build (CMakeLists.txt, main.cpp, flutter_window.cpp, etc.) or use flutter create to generate them
Step 18: Create README.md with app description, features, and build instructions
acceptance_criteria: pubspec.yaml contains all required dependencies (http, flutter_tts, speech_to_text, provider)
Translation service correctly calls MyMemory API and parses response
UI has a large prominent speak/microphone button as the central element
Language selectors default to English -> Vietnamese
All supported languages are available in the language selector
GitHub Actions workflow file exists and builds for Web, Android, and Windows
GitHub Actions workflow creates a release with artifacts on successful build
verification: Verify the project structure is complete with all necessary files for a Flutter project
Verify the GitHub Actions workflow YAML is valid
Verify the Dart code has no syntax errors by checking with dart analyze (after Flutter is installed)
Push to main and monitor the GitHub Actions build
findings: All project files created from scratch. Flutter is not installed in the sandbox so local build/test was not possible; YAML validity confirmed with Python parser. Dart brace balance verified. The project uses standard Flutter template patterns for Android and Windows platform configs. The CI workflow uses subosito/flutter-action@v2 with stable channel and creates releases via softprops/action-gh-release@v1. Android build uses debug signing for release mode to avoid keystore requirement. The speak_button widget uses Flutter built-in AnimatedBuilder for the pulse animation.
